old_value:
{"id":"CF-LIBS-ak3.1","title":"Spectral ID physical validity + synthetic-first debugging","description":"# Spectral ID physical validity + synthetic-first debugging

## Problem
Element identification quality is not yet reliable on real data. Before further threshold tuning, we need to prove that the synthetic forward model is physically consistent and usable as trusted ground truth.

## Objectives
1. Verify physical correctness of LIBS spectrum synthesis (population model, emissivity scaling, line shapes, broadening, instrument convolution, and units).
2. Build a reproducible synthetic benchmark corpus with known ground truth labels and controlled perturbations.
3. Use synthetic-first debugging to isolate failure modes in ALIAS, Comb, and Correlation peak-ID pipelines.
4. Validate tuned algorithms on real spectra without regressing false-positive controls.

## Scope
- Forward model physics audit against standard LIBS equations and NIST ASD line behavior.
- Deterministic synthetic data generation for pure and mixed compositions.
- Stress tests for wavelength miscalibration (global shift + non-linear warps), line broadening, SNR, and baseline/continuum effects.
- Element-level and peak-level benchmark metrics with strict reproducibility.

## Deliverables
- Physics-validation report with pass/fail checks and residual plots.
- Synthetic benchmark harness + datasets + manifests.
- Identifier debug report (per-method failure taxonomy and root causes).
- Updated default thresholds/configs with before/after benchmark tables.
- Real-data confirmation report tied to synthetic-derived settings.

## Exit Criteria
- Synthetic pure-element datasets are correctly identified across target perturbation ranges.
- Mixture datasets recover present elements with bounded false positives.
- Real-data benchmark improves vs baseline and meets agreed acceptance gates.

new_value: Closed
Physics invariants pytest suite complete: 17 tests across 6 classes (NonNegativeIntensity, BoltzmannLineRatio, PathLengthScaling, PartitionFunction, IonizationBalance, UnitConsistency). All passing.

Consolidated validation runner (scripts/run_nist_validation.py) and baseline report committed. Covers partition functions vs NIST ASD, ionization fractions vs NIST LIBS Simulation, and spectral comparison artifacts. Report at output/validation/nist_crosscheck_report.json.

Calibration stress analysis script and report committed. Findings: Correlation is robust to +/-1nm shift (10.5% F1 loss), ALIAS needs careful calibration (22.7% loss), Comb most sensitive (30.7% loss). Warp analysis deferred — needs expanded corpus. Recommendations: global shift sufficient for Correlation, higher-order correction for ALIAS/Comb.
Threshold Pareto analysis script and report committed (1cad3fe)

old_value: {"id":"CF-LIBS-6pm","title":"[HIGH] Validate i64→u8/u32/usize casts in Rhai API to prevent wrap on negative input","description":"Rhai passes all integers as i64. Unchecked casts at libs_bindings.rs:214, 230, 361, 440, 490 silently wrap negative script input into huge values — can send junk hardware commands or trigger huge allocations in get_calibration. Fix: use u8::try_from(grating).map_err(rhai_error)?, u32::try_from(n).map_err(rhai_error)?, etc. at all Rhai-facing cast sites.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:03Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:03Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Parent epic: CF-LIBS-ggc (LIBS Code Quality \u0026 Safety Hardening)"}
old_value: {"id":"CF-LIBS-bbq","title":"[HIGH] Convert panic-path factory functions to return Result in libs_bindings.rs","description":"Factory closures at libs_bindings.rs:525, 528, 537, 540 use .expect() and unconditional Handle::current()/block_in_place — panics instead of recoverable script errors. Fix: return Result\u003cDynamic, Box\u003cEvalAltResult\u003e\u003e from register_fn closures, route all errors through rhai_error(), avoid assuming Tokio runtime presence.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:03Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:03Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Parent epic: CF-LIBS-ggc (LIBS Code Quality \u0026 Safety Hardening)"}
old_value: {"id":"CF-LIBS-86u","title":"[HIGH] Fix clippy gate failures from lossy numeric casts in libs_benchmarks.rs and mock.rs","description":"cargo clippy --all-targets fails due to lossy as-casts under workspace pedantic lints. Sites: libs_benchmarks.rs:29,38 (as f64 from usize/u32), mock.rs:65,72,335 (as f64 from integer types). Fix: replace with f64::from() where lossless, or add #[allow(clippy::cast_precision_loss)] with justification comment where precision loss is intentional and bounded.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:03Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:03Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Parent epic: CF-LIBS-ggc (LIBS Code Quality \u0026 Safety Hardening)"}
old_value: {"id":"CF-LIBS-xnc","title":"[MED] Convert linear_interp assert! panics to Result in public API","description":"linear_interp() at radiance_calibration.rs:100 uses assert_eq!/assert! to validate input — panics on malformed input instead of returning a structured error. This is a public library function. Fix: change signature to fn linear_interp(src_x: \u0026[f64], src_y: \u0026[f64], target_x: \u0026[f64]) -\u003e Result\u003cVec\u003cf64\u003e, CalibrationError\u003e and propagate through RadianceCalibrator::calibrate().","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:18Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:18Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Parent epic: CF-LIBS-ggc (LIBS Code Quality \u0026 Safety Hardening)"}
old_value: {"id":"CF-LIBS-owz","title":"[MED] Harden calibration file parser against NaN wavelengths and delimiter edge cases","description":"radiance_calibration.rs:157 uses splitn which can produce empty tokens from consecutive delimiters; radiance_calibration.rs:182 sort_by uses partial_cmp which can produce unstable ordering for NaN wavelengths. Fix: filter empty tokens after split, reject non-finite wl/val with early continue, use f64::total_cmp in sort_by after filtering ensures no NaN survives.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:18Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:18Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Parent epic: CF-LIBS-ggc (LIBS Code Quality \u0026 Safety Hardening)"}
old_value: {"id":"CF-LIBS-gqg","title":"[MED] Make MockCamera::start_stream idempotent — prevent duplicate producer tasks","description":"MockCamera::start_stream at mock.rs:372,377 spawns a new Tokio task on every call with no guard. Calling start_stream twice produces duplicate frame generators and noisy observers. Fix: use AtomicBool::compare_exchange to gate task spawn, store JoinHandle in Option\u003cJoinHandle\u003e, abort handle in stop_stream.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:18Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:18Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Parent epic: CF-LIBS-ggc (LIBS Code Quality \u0026 Safety Hardening)"}
old_value: {"id":"CF-LIBS-5uo","title":"[MED] Extract shared LIBS spectrum generator to eliminate duplication between bench and mock","description":"libs_benchmarks.rs:21 contains a local bench_generate_libs_spectrum() that mirrors mock.rs:56 generate_libs_spectrum(). These will drift. Fix: move the canonical generator into common::processing (or a test-utils module) as a #[cfg(any(test, bench))] function and have both sites call it. This also makes benchmark numbers actually representative of mock behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:18Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:18Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Parent epic: CF-LIBS-ggc (LIBS Code Quality \u0026 Safety Hardening)"}
old_value: {"id":"CF-LIBS-zcu","title":"[LOW] Replace tautological test assertion in libs_bindings.rs:686 with concrete invariant","description":"Test at libs_bindings.rs:686 contains an assertion that passes regardless of behavior (tautological check). This gives false confidence. Fix: replace with a concrete invariant check — e.g. assert the actual calibrated output value equals an analytically expected result given known lamp/cal inputs, or verify wavelength count matches input.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:28Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:28Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Parent epic: CF-LIBS-ggc (LIBS Code Quality \u0026 Safety Hardening)"}
old_value: {"id":"CF-LIBS-80c","title":"[LOW] Fuse intermediate correction Vec allocation in RadianceCalibrator::calibrate hot path","description":"radiance_calibration.rs:271 allocates an intermediate correction: Vec\u003cf64\u003e then iterates over it again to produce calibrated. This is a 2-pass allocation in what will be a hot path for scan loops. Fix: compute in one fused iterator — zip lamp_interp, cal_interp, spectrum.values in one .map() chain directly into calibrated: Vec\u003cf64\u003e. Reduces one Vec allocation per calibrate() call.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:28Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:28:28Z"}

old_value:
{"id":"CF-LIBS-1tx","title":"CF-LIBS v1.0 Production Release","description":"# CF-LIBS v1.0 Production Release

Master epic consolidating all remaining work for a production-ready CF-LIBS release.

## Based on NotebookLM Analysis

### Validated Strengths (Complete)
- ✓ Classic CF-LIBS algorithm (Ciucci et al. methodology)
- ✓ Saha-Boltzmann equilibrium with Irwin partition functions
- ✓ Voigt profiles (Humlicek W4 + Weideman JAX)
- ✓ Bayesian UQ with NumPyro MCMC and dynesty nested sampling
- ✓ Hybrid inversion (manifold + L-BFGS)
- ✓ JAX acceleration for GPU computing

### Identified Gaps (This Epic)
1. **Self-Absorption Complexity**: Current recursive correction may not capture full optically thick plasma complexity (COG/CD-SB methods needed)
2. **Ecosystem Integration**: Jupyter visualization, hardware drivers, export tools
3. **Matrix Effect Correction**: Beyond standard CF-LIBS assumptions
4. **Error Propagation**: Monte Carlo uncertainty for complex samples

## Sub-Epics
1. **Phase 4a**: Implementation Backlog (P1-P2 ready tasks)
2. **Phase 4b**: Ecosystem & Integration (visualization, drivers, export)
3. **Phase 4c**: Physics Refinements (self-absorption, matrix effects)
4. **Phase 4d**: Research Roadmap (P3-P4 future work)

## Success Criteria
- All P1/P2 tasks complete with tests
- Comprehensive documentation and examples
- Production-quality error handling
- Published API reference
- Integration tests with real LIBS data","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:54:26Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:54:26Z"}

Scope:
- Integrate Phase 2 architecture/reliability outcomes and Phase 3 quality hardening
- Validate LIBS workflows on real hardware and Windows LIBS machine
- Ensure scripting + calibration + mock/driver behavior are aligned and documented

Key files / areas:
- crates/scripting/src/libs_bindings.rs
- crates/common/src/processing/radiance_calibration.rs
- crates/driver-andor-sdk3/src/mock.rs
- crates/server/src/**
- scripts/build-maitai.sh, scripts/env-check.sh

Acceptance criteria:
- All P1 blockers in Phases 2 and 3 are closed
- Real-hardware validation matrix completed with report artifacts
- No blocking clippy/test regressions on LIBS-related crates
- Residual risks captured as explicit Phase-5 backlog issues"}

{"id":"CF-LIBS-1tx","title":"Phase 4: LIBS Integration Delivery (Release Gate)","description":"# CF-LIBS v1.0 Production Release

Master epic consolidating all remaining work for a production-ready CF-LIBS release.

## Based on NotebookLM Analysis

### Validated Strengths (Complete)
- ✓ Classic CF-LIBS algorithm (Ciucci et al. methodology)
- ✓ Saha-Boltzmann equilibrium with Irwin partition functions
- ✓ Voigt profiles (Humlicek W4 + Weideman JAX)
- ✓ Bayesian UQ with NumPyro MCMC and dynesty nested sampling
- ✓ Hybrid inversion (manifold + L-BFGS)
- ✓ JAX acceleration for GPU computing

### Identified Gaps (This Epic)
1. **Self-Absorption Complexity**: Current recursive correction may not capture full optically thick plasma complexity (COG/CD-SB methods needed)
2. **Ecosystem Integration**: Jupyter visualization, hardware drivers, export tools
3. **Matrix Effect Correction**: Beyond standard CF-LIBS assumptions
4. **Error Propagation**: Monte Carlo uncertainty for complex samples

## Sub-Epics
1. **Phase 4a**: Implementation Backlog (P1-P2 ready tasks)
2. **Phase 4b**: Ecosystem & Integration (visualization, drivers, export)
3. **Phase 4c**: Physics Refinements (self-absorption, matrix effects)
4. **Phase 4d**: Research Roadmap (P3-P4 future work)

## Success Criteria
- All P1/P2 tasks complete with tests
- Comprehensive documentation and examples
- Production-quality error handling
- Published API reference
- Integration tests with real LIBS data","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:54:26Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:35:19Z"}
